sections.paragraph:
κολοβόω 1 aor. ἐκολόβωσα. Pass.: 1 fut. κολοβωθήσομαι; 1 aor. ἐκολοβώθην; pf. ptc. κεκολοβωμένος (s. prec. entry; Aristot., HA 1, 1, 487b, 24; Diod S 1, 78, 5; 2 Km 4:12 ‘cut off, cut short’; GrBar 9:7; Ar.13, 2) ‘mutilate, curtail’ (so Polyb. 1, 80, 13; Epict. 2, 10, 20).

① to cause someth. not to be full length, to shorten, lit., of stones κεκολοβωμένοι short Hv 3, 2, 8; 3, 6, 4 (s. NBrox, Der Hirt des Hermas ’91, 135). ὅλως ἐξ αὐτῶν οὐδὲν ἐκολοβώθη nothing at all was too short about them Hs 9, 8, 5.

② to reduce the duration of someth., shorten, fig. extension of 1: the last days Mt 24:22ab; Mk 13:20ab (cp. GrBar 9:7 θεὸς … ἐκολόβωσεν τὰς ἡμέρας αὐτῆς [i.e. τῆς σελήνης]).—DELG s.v. κόλος. M-M. TW.

aor. aor. = aorist

fut. fut. = fut.

  1. pf. = perfect

ptc. ptc. = participle

prec. prec. = preceding

Aristot Aristot , various works, IV b.c.—List 5

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

GrBar GrBar = Greek Baruch (Apocalypsis Baruchi Graece)—List 2

Ar. Ar. = Aristides, apologist, II a.d.—List 5

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

someth. someth. = something

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

Hv Hv = Visions

Hermas Hermas , s. H—List 1

Hs Hs = Similitudes

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

i.e.

i.e. = id est (that is)

I.-E. Indo-European

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 556
